Context class is orthogonal to status.\end{minipage}\par\vspace{0.25em}

\hline
\textbf{Field} & \textbf{Record}\\
\hline
Phase status & \cellcolor{green!18}G\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Context class & BaselinePhase\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Phase documentation tier & F3\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Phase documentation depth requirement & Compact record: what exists, why it exists, owning phase, source location, and whether it may affect Lean objects. Avoid proofbook bloat.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Record change version & \\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Record change timestamp & \\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Implementation status & completed\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Preparation status & completed-baseline-freeze\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Phase type & baseline\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Phase-type-specific contract & Freeze/\allowbreak{}reference phase: record source package and do not introduce new code.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Title /\allowbreak{} state & implemented /\allowbreak{} closed\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Preparation workflow & No source-preparation workflow was needed. The phase freezes the last locally green SM9h package as immutable baseline evidence.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Source-to-CNNA mapping & Existing SM9h modules are not renamed in the baseline. They are mapped as legacy derivation sites and later wrapped by neutral target modules.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Dependency-depth audit & No new dependencies are introduced. Baseline freeze depends only on user-confirmed green SM9h code and static package inspection.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Phase split decision & No split. This is a governance freeze, not a mathematical implementation phase.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Positive path plan & Preserve the green baseline and build neutral wrappers later instead of mutating historical modules.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Refactor regression protocol & Not applicable unless phase\_type is refactor or the phase changes imports/\allowbreak{}declarations. If refactor applies: wrapper-first, bounded migration package, local full-build evidence before closeout.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Refactor migration metrics & Not recorded yet. Refactor phases must fill declarations\_migrated\_count, imports\_changed, import\_graph\_delta, consumer\_breakage\_audit, and legacy/\allowbreak{}coexistence status before implementation.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Lean/\allowbreak{}mathlib version policy & Baseline is Lean 4.28.0/\allowbreak{}mathlib snapshot unless an explicit phase-boundary upgrade gate is opened. New imports must be verified against the chosen baseline;\newline noncomputable/\allowbreak{}classical pressure must be logged and isolated.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
ExecComplex boundary policy & Apply EXEC-2 when relevant: operational ExecComplex remains Q x Q;\newline algebraic irrational data stays mirror-only unless a rational/\allowbreak{}integer invariant is separately proved.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Consumer-contract direction & No special cross-phase consumer contract recorded yet. If a later phase consumes this output, record export shape and import direction before implementation.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Governance-overhead review & Pending until first real refactor/\allowbreak{}code-change package closes;\newline then record which governance gates prevented concrete errors and whether later phases can be flatter.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Proof rehearsal before implementation & Not a Lean proof phase. The rehearsal is architectural: treat SM9h as immutable input and verify that later phases consume it through neutral interfaces.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Expected tactics & None;\newline no Lean proofs are added in phase 0.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Expected mathlib imports & None added.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
ExecComplex/\allowbreak{}computable plan & ExecComplex baseline is preserved;\newline no analytic or mirror replacement is introduced.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Concrete computable instance traversal & Infrastructure-only or not prepared yet. Before operational code implementation, name a concrete finite instance and the path from input to output.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Computable path non-skeleton status & not-assessed-yet;\newline must be explicit before operational implementation\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Expected executable witness & not specified yet\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Mathlib complication log & No phase-specific mathlib complication recorded yet.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Noncomputable boundary audit & No phase-specific noncomputable-boundary audit recorded yet.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Mathlib mitigation/\allowbreak{}refactor layer & If mathlib introduces noncomputable/\allowbreak{}classical pressure, isolate in allowed mirror/\allowbreak{}non-operational layer or backjump to the earliest natural source layer.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Bridge gap status & No bridge gap is closed here. Known gaps are registered for later phases.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Natural backjump/\allowbreak{}refactor layer & If a later neutral wrapper cannot consume SM9h cleanly, backjump to the neutral interface phase, not to this frozen baseline.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Implementation prompt contract & Use the standardized positive-path implementation workflow: respect all plan sections, import/\allowbreak{}consumer discipline, derived-only and computable operational path, no forbidden shortcuts, package the full modified code, and verify plan points after implementation.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Global no-go inheritance & yes;\newline all global\_no\_go\_guardrails inherited (GNG01-GNG24)\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
How implemented & The SM9h code package is treated as a frozen baseline. No new Lean declarations are introduced in this phase. The existing historical modules remain unchanged and are referenced only as legacy derivation sites. Part 2 will add neutral wrapper/\allowbreak{}import surfaces later instead of renaming or editing the frozen baseline directly.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Verification /\allowbreak{} evidence & Baseline object is the provided green SM9h package. Current derivation sites include CNNA/\allowbreak{}PillarA/\allowbreak{}Arithmetic/\allowbreak{}BuildAll.lean, Smoke/\allowbreak{}* through GeneratedOperationalQuadraticDiscriminantSourceFromSM9g1Window.lean, plus the already existing Schur, DtN, Finite, Handoff, and Cayley-Dickson legacy modules. I did not run Lean in this environment;\newline the green status is taken from the supplied/\allowbreak{}local confirmed baseline and static inspection.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Post-implementation plan check & Closed as governance baseline: no code mutation, no new formulas, no new consumer access, no Lean build run by assistant.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Artifact packaging rule & Return a complete zip with all modified files and generated planning artifacts when applicable. Lean build success may be recorded only from an actual current build or from the user-reported local build;\newline otherwise state that local Lean build is required.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Implementation notes & This record is deliberately non-code: it documents freeze semantics. Any later refactor must preserve import compatibility and must not mutate the Part 1 baseline.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Source materials /\allowbreak{} references & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item user-confirmed green SM9h package\item static inspection of v17 roadmap data and legacy code-module references\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Extracted established theory objects & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item ExecComplex operational matrix/\allowbreak{}discriminant baseline\item legacy Schur/\allowbreak{}DtN source chain\item legacy Handoff/\allowbreak{}Finite/\allowbreak{}AQFT/\allowbreak{}Cayley-Dickson reference surfaces\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
No-go guardrails & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item GLOBAL-INHERITED: top-level global\_no\_go\_guardrails apply;\newline this phase list is local addenda only.\item no @[simp] attributes in the operational path\item no noncomputable in the operational path\item no classical helper unless isolated in allowed mirror/\allowbreak{}non-operational layer\item no Matrix.inv or external inverse oracle in operational derivations\item no free formula without proof-carrying entry structure\item no consumer-side patch when the missing structure belongs earlier\item no premature obstruction before a serious positive construction attempt\item no phase-semantic public API names\item no fragile rewrite chain when a structural constructor/\allowbreak{}projection proof is available\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
\end{longtable}

\subsection*{Phase 1.1.1 -- Pre-code governance umbrella: module manifest, reuse, consumption classification, proofbook infrastructure, blue correction, API and pruning gates}
\label{impl:phase-1-1-1}

Context class is orthogonal to status.\end{minipage}\par\vspace{0.25em}

\hline

Record change version & \\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Record change timestamp & \\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Implementation status & completed\\

Preparation status & decomposition-closed\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Phase type & documentation\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Phase-type-specific contract & Documentation phase: record dossier coverage, verification/\allowbreak{}audit status, and links to code/\allowbreak{}object records;\newline no mathematical object is generated by documentation alone.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Title /\allowbreak{} state & Consumption classification and blue-object correction umbrella\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Preparation workflow & Phase-bound preparation from the single YAML master. Collect only source material needed by this phase;\newline register later-phase material as future/\allowbreak{}missing-source rather than placing it here.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Source-to-CNNA mapping & This umbrella binds LEG0/\allowbreak{}LEG5/\allowbreak{}LEG6/\allowbreak{}LEG7/\allowbreak{}LEG8/\allowbreak{}LEG9. It prevents code moves before consumption and semantic API roles are classified.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Dependency-depth audit & Too broad for one implementation step;\newline split into 1.1.1.1-1.1.1.5.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Phase split decision & Split required by v23: classification, blue correction ledger, public API contract, pruning ledger, generated-view lock.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Positive path plan & Proceed through child gates only. No deletion, wrapper move, or status quarantine until classification and contracts exist.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Refactor regression protocol & Not applicable unless phase\_type is refactor or the phase changes imports/\allowbreak{}declarations. If refactor applies: wrapper-first, bounded migration package, local full-build evidence before closeout.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Refactor migration metrics & Not recorded yet. Refactor phases must fill declarations\_migrated\_count, imports\_changed, import\_graph\_delta, consumer\_breakage\_audit, and legacy/\allowbreak{}coexistence status before implementation.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Lean/\allowbreak{}mathlib version policy & Baseline is Lean 4.28.0/\allowbreak{}mathlib snapshot unless an explicit phase-boundary upgrade gate is opened. New imports must be verified against the chosen baseline;\newline noncomputable/\allowbreak{}classical pressure must be logged and isolated.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
ExecComplex boundary policy & Apply EXEC-2 when relevant: operational ExecComplex remains Q x Q;\newline algebraic irrational data stays mirror-only unless a rational/\allowbreak{}integer invariant is separately proved.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Consumer-contract direction & No special cross-phase consumer contract recorded yet. If a later phase consumes this output, record export shape and import direction before implementation.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Governance-overhead review & Pending until first real refactor/\allowbreak{}code-change package closes;\newline then record which governance gates prevented concrete errors and whether later phases can be flatter.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Proof rehearsal before implementation & No Lean proof. Structural validation only: exactly one active child, complete scratchpad records, generated tables/\allowbreak{}PDF compile.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Expected tactics & No Lean tactics expected. If a small Lean audit module is later added, use only constants/\allowbreak{}strings/\allowbreak{}records with exact/\allowbreak{}rfl where applicable.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Expected mathlib imports & No new mathlib imports. No Lean imports are required for this planning/\allowbreak{}audit gate.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
ExecComplex/\allowbreak{}computable plan & No ExecComplex object is introduced unless the phase explicitly touches O00;\newline preserve existing computability boundaries.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Concrete computable instance traversal & Infrastructure-only or not prepared yet. Before operational code implementation, name a concrete finite instance and the path from input to output.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Computable path non-skeleton status & not-assessed-yet;\newline must be explicit before operational implementation\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Expected executable witness & not specified yet\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Mathlib complication log & No phase-specific mathlib complication recorded yet.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Noncomputable boundary audit & No phase-specific noncomputable-boundary audit recorded yet.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Mathlib mitigation/\allowbreak{}refactor layer & If mathlib introduces noncomputable/\allowbreak{}classical pressure, isolate in allowed mirror/\allowbreak{}non-operational layer or backjump to the earliest natural source layer.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Bridge gap status & No mathematical bridge expected;\newline missing names/\allowbreak{}imports must be localized to the natural source/\allowbreak{}split layer.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Natural backjump/\allowbreak{}refactor layer & Backjump to the earliest natural split bucket: inventory, Legacy.StatusGates, CoreSymbols, Legacy.PhaseAliases, or import firewall.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Implementation prompt contract & Use the standardized positive-path implementation workflow: respect all plan sections, import/\allowbreak{}consumer discipline, derived-only and computable operational path, no forbidden shortcuts, package the full modified code, and verify plan points after implementation.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Global no-go inheritance & yes;\newline all global\_no\_go\_guardrails inherited (GNG01-GNG24)\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
How implemented & Planning/\allowbreak{}decomposition closure only: the child DAG, ordering rule, and active cursor were fixed in the YAML master. No Lean refactor or theorem implementation is claimed for this umbrella row.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Verification /\allowbreak{} evidence & Generated YAML/\allowbreak{}table/\allowbreak{}PDF validation for the governance decomposition. Lean build was not run in this environment.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Build result & No Lean build run;\newline governance/\allowbreak{}documentation-only closure.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Post-implementation plan check & Satisfied as a routing/\allowbreak{}order lock: no white predecessor remains on the active branch before the unique yellow active phase.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Artifact packaging rule & Return a complete zip with all modified files and generated planning artifacts when applicable. Lean build success may be recorded only from an actual current build or from the user-reported local build;\newline otherwise state that local Lean build is required.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Implementation notes & Green here means decomposition-closed umbrella. Descendant implementation phases remain responsible for code movement and proof closure.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Source materials /\allowbreak{} references & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item masters/\allowbreak{}cnna\_roadmap\_master.yaml v23\item repo\_inventory/\allowbreak{}raw\_export\item object register blue rows\item static declaration/\allowbreak{}reference audit\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Extracted established theory objects & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item consumption classification gate\item blue-object semantic correction gate\item public API contract gate\item pruning ledger gate\item generated-view authority lock\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
No-go guardrails & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item GLOBAL-INHERITED: top-level global\_no\_go\_guardrails apply;\newline this phase list is local addenda only.\item no @[simp] attributes in the operational path\item no noncomputable in the operational path\item no classical helper unless isolated in allowed mirror/\allowbreak{}non-operational layer\item no Matrix.inv or external inverse oracle in operational derivations\item no free formula without proof-carrying entry structure\item no consumer-side patch when the missing structure belongs earlier\item no premature obstruction before a serious positive construction attempt\item no phase-semantic public API names\item no fragile rewrite chain when a structural constructor/\allowbreak{}projection proof is available\item do not delete legacy status enums during quarantine\item do not let Phase 3+ import Legacy.StatusGates\item do not treat old TeX/\allowbreak{}PDF files as maintained roadmap sources\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
\end{longtable}

\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Preparation workflow & Infrastructure gate inserted after global no-go inheritance and before active module-manifest implementation.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Source-to-CNNA mapping & LEG14 and LEG15 are workflow/\allowbreak{}control objects. They do not add CNNA mathematics;\newline they force future operational phases to expose executable instance traversal and mathlib computability risks before code edits.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Dependency-depth audit & Depends only on closed 1.1.1.1.0 global no-go inheritance. It must be green before active phase 1.1.1.1.1 because module manifests must know whether planned code is infrastructure-only or operational/\allowbreak{}executable.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Phase split decision & New small predecessor gate added. Do not split further unless a separate generated static-audit table is later introduced.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Positive path plan & Keep positive construction first, but require instance pressure: generated substrate/\allowbreak{}window -> operational declarations -> concrete endpoint witness. If the path cannot be traversed, record the earliest natural missing source layer instead of patching the consumer.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Module implementation manifest & No code modules introduced by this gate.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Semantic placement decision & Governance belongs in YAML master and generated views unless a future code-level policy module is justified.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Reuse and duplication audit & No duplicate implementation authorized;\newline decision reduces future duplication risk.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Canonical generator policy & Generator-like logic must consume the canonical smoke/\allowbreak{}theory generator path;\newline no parallel implementation.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Module compactness rule & Do not split code solely for this planning decision;\newline use YAML/\allowbreak{}workflow unless a reusable code-level policy module is later justified.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Refactor regression protocol & Not applicable unless phase\_type is refactor or the phase changes imports/\allowbreak{}declarations. If refactor applies: wrapper-first, bounded migration package, local full-build evidence before closeout.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Refactor migration metrics & Not recorded yet. Refactor phases must fill declarations\_migrated\_count, imports\_changed, import\_graph\_delta, consumer\_breakage\_audit, and legacy/\allowbreak{}coexistence status before implementation.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Lean/\allowbreak{}mathlib version policy & Baseline is Lean 4.28.0/\allowbreak{}mathlib snapshot unless an explicit phase-boundary upgrade gate is opened. New imports must be verified against the chosen baseline;\newline noncomputable/\allowbreak{}classical pressure must be logged and isolated.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
ExecComplex boundary policy & Apply EXEC-2 when relevant: operational ExecComplex remains Q x Q;\newline algebraic irrational data stays mirror-only unless a rational/\allowbreak{}integer invariant is separately proved.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Consumer-contract direction & No special cross-phase consumer contract recorded yet. If a later phase consumes this output, record export shape and import direction before implementation.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Governance-overhead review & Pending until first real refactor/\allowbreak{}code-change package closes;\newline then record which governance gates prevented concrete errors and whether later phases can be flatter.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Proof rehearsal before implementation & For a future implementation: choose concrete finite instance;\newline compute through the intended operational path;\newline compare endpoint shape to proof target;\newline if mathlib blocks executability, isolate mirror use or backjump.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Expected tactics & No Lean tactics now. Future phases should prefer constructor/\allowbreak{}projection proofs and explicit finite-instance lemmas over fragile rewrite chains.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Expected mathlib imports & None for this planning gate. Future phases must record concrete mathlib import candidates and computability status.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
ExecComplex/\allowbreak{}computable plan & No ExecComplex object introduced. Future ExecComplex phases must include a concrete finite instance traversal through the ExecComplex boundary/\allowbreak{}mirror seam.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Concrete computable instance traversal & Infrastructure-only gate: no operational Lean object introduced. The generated requirement is itself the traversal contract for future operational phases.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Computable path non-skeleton status & closed for planning infrastructure;\newline future operational phases must state concrete instance traversal before code edits\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Expected executable witness & Required future field;\newline for this gate the witness is generated scratchpad visibility, not a Lean executable term.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Mathlib complication log & Planning gate only. Records known risk class: mathlib algebraic interfaces may force noncomputable/\allowbreak{}classical dependencies;\newline future phases must log concrete module/\allowbreak{}API names.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Noncomputable boundary audit & No Lean code changed. Future operational phases must separate operational code from mirror/\allowbreak{}non-operational noncomputable uses.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Mathlib mitigation/\allowbreak{}refactor layer & If mathlib forces noncomputability in operational route, isolate in mirror layer or derive/\allowbreak{}implement an operational replacement at the natural source layer.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Bridge gap status & Closed as planning infrastructure. Mathematical bridges remain phase-specific.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Natural backjump/\allowbreak{}refactor layer & If concrete traversal fails because an earlier generated datum is missing, backjump to the earliest natural generator/\allowbreak{}source layer. If mathlib only provides noncomputable APIs, route them to mirror/\allowbreak{}non-operational layer or build an operational replacement.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Implementation prompt contract & Before any Lean code edit, report whether the phase is infrastructure-only or operational. If operational, provide concrete\_instance\_traversal and expected\_executable\_witness, plus mathlib\_complication\_log/\allowbreak{}noncomputable\_boundary\_audit.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Global no-go inheritance & yes;\newline all global\_no\_go\_guardrails inherited (GNG01-GNG24)\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
How implemented & Planning infrastructure added to the YAML master and generator: every scratchpad record now contains concrete-instance traversal and mathlib/\allowbreak{}noncomputable complication fields. No Lean code moved.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Verification /\allowbreak{} evidence & generate\_tables.py validates the new required fields and renders them in implementation\_scratchpad.tex;\newline artifact generation only, no Lean build.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Proof strategy & No Lean proof. Infrastructure proof is that the generator requires and renders the fields for every phase record.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Build result & No Lean build run;\newline generated planning artifacts only.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Post-implementation plan check & After this gate, active phase 1.1.1.1.1 inherits the fields and must keep them visible in the scratchpad.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Artifact packaging rule & Return a complete zip with all modified files and generated planning artifacts. Lean build success may be recorded only from actual/\allowbreak{}current build or user-reported local build;\newline otherwise local Lean build is required.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Implementation notes & This closes the workflow-contract gap: operational computable code must be exercised by concrete instances and mathlib barriers must be logged before implementation.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Source materials /\allowbreak{} references & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item prior SM/\allowbreak{}AR implementation history\item global\_no\_go\_guardrails\item implementation\_scratchpad records\item mathlib computability complication examples from earlier polynomial/\allowbreak{}matrix attempts\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]

\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]

\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Forbidden patterns checked & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item global no-go inheritance retained\item operational noncomputable/\allowbreak{}classical risk must be logged\item no Matrix.inv/\allowbreak{}external inverse oracle weakening\item no skeleton-only operational closeout\end{itemize}\\

Complex coordinates, tau, sqrt(D), and analytic j evaluation remain mirror/\allowbreak{}comparison.\\

\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Noncomputable boundary audit & Operational tests must avoid noncomputable analytic complex evaluation. Mirror tests may state comparison targets but must not feed operational definitions.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Mathlib mitigation/\allowbreak{}refactor layer & Use rational/\allowbreak{}integer surrogates in operational layer;\newline isolate analytic or algebraic-irrational material in mirror/\allowbreak{}profile modules.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Bridge gap status & Open until L06/\allowbreak{}L04/\allowbreak{}M05 and the generator route are separately closed.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Natural backjump/\allowbreak{}refactor layer & If tests require new generator semantics, backjump to the canonical generator layer;\newline do not patch the test module.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Implementation prompt contract & Before code: provide module manifest, reuse audit, concrete finite instances, and test matrix rows;\newline do not create duplicate generators.\\

\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Post-implementation plan check & After implementation, verify the test matrix uses code-defined branching and did not duplicate generator logic.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Artifact packaging rule & Return complete zip;\newline no Lean-build success may be claimed unless actually run.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Source materials /\allowbreak{} references & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item TomS thread post \#97 /\allowbreak{} antaris b-ary tree Eisenstein-Gauss heuristic construction\item Existing EXEC-2 rational-boundary policy\item Canonical generator/\allowbreak{}reuse policy\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Extracted established theory objects & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item b-ary address words over code-defined branching\item angle profiles 120 degrees /\allowbreak{} 90 degrees\item closure-depth targets L=3 /\allowbreak{} L=4\item CM targets rho and i\item j targets 0 and 1728\item hierarchical-vs-spiral embedding distinction\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Modified modules & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item masters/\allowbreak{}cnna\_roadmap\_master.yaml\item generated tables/\allowbreak{}PDF views\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Forbidden patterns checked & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item no generator semantics imported from angle drawings\item no noncomputable analytic j in operational path\item no duplicate b-ary generator implementation\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
No-go guardrails & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item Inherit global guardrails.\item Do not use geometric heuristic as generator input.\item Do not put irrational tau/\allowbreak{}coordinates in the operational ExecComplex path.\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
\end{longtable}

\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Title /\allowbreak{} state & General early finite computation test-matrix contract and non-specialization lock\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Preparation workflow & General finite computation-test workflow;\newline extends the concrete computable instance traversal workflow.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Source-to-CNNA mapping & The post-97 Eisenstein/\allowbreak{}Gauss rows map to sentinel rows;\newline the governing CNNA object is now the general finite computation test matrix O07. L09/\allowbreak{}L10/\allowbreak{}M08/\allowbreak{}BR10 remain specialized later consumers.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Dependency-depth audit & Must precede Phase 1.1.1.1.1 because module manifests must decide where generic tests live before code is written. It does not depend on later CM/\allowbreak{}j phases except for sentinel comparison columns.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Phase split decision & Added as its own green infrastructure gate because the requirement changes test semantics globally, not only Phase 8.5.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Positive path plan & Use generic tests first: construct finite generated instances, run the operational/\allowbreak{}computable path, export rational/\allowbreak{}integer/\allowbreak{}finite witnesses, then attach sentinel rows such as Eisenstein/\allowbreak{}Gauss as comparison rows where dependencies permit.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Refactor regression protocol & No code movement here. Later refactors must keep generic test rows buildable as migration sentinels.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Refactor migration metrics & Future refactor phases must report declarations migrated plus generic/\allowbreak{}sentinel test rows still passing.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Lean/\allowbreak{}mathlib version policy & Version impacts are logged per test field. A mathlib upgrade may be required only at phase boundaries, never inside a half-migrated test harness.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
ExecComplex boundary policy & EXEC-2 binding: generic operational fields are finite/\allowbreak{}rational/\allowbreak{}integer;\newline non-rational geometry remains mirror/\allowbreak{}comparison.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Consumer-contract direction & Tests consume canonical generator and operational profiles. They do not define generator semantics.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Governance-overhead review & This gate is justified only if it prevents special-case tunnel vision during the first computation phases;\newline review after first real code-test package.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Proof rehearsal before implementation & A future test closes only if at least one generic row family is generated from code-defined data, not hand-coded to the expected CM result. Sentinel rows may fail without invalidating the generic test harness;\newline generic rows failing blocks promotion of the computable path.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Expected tactics & Not a Lean proof phase. Future Lean tests should prefer explicit constructors, finite enumerations, and proof-carrying row records over rewriting chains.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Expected mathlib imports & No new mathlib import now. Future test modules must log whether finite matrix, polynomial, or number-theory APIs force noncomputable pressure.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
ExecComplex/\allowbreak{}computable plan & Operational test fields remain finite/\allowbreak{}rational/\allowbreak{}integer. Algebraic coordinates, tau, sqrt(D), analytic j, and non-rational angles are mirror/\allowbreak{}comparison fields only.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Concrete computable instance traversal & Defines the global obligation: every operational computable phase must include or point to finite generated rows that actually traverse the path, not only typecheck a skeleton.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Computable path non-skeleton status & strengthened: a path is non-skeletal only when a concrete finite input produces a proof-carrying executable witness or an explicitly documented failure row.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Expected executable witness & Generic row witness: finite input record -> output record with rational/\allowbreak{}integer/\allowbreak{}finite fields and proof/\allowbreak{}status flags;\newline sentinel comparison fields are separate.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Mathlib complication log & Future rows must log mathlib/\allowbreak{}noncomputable barriers per field, especially matrix inverses, polynomial APIs, discriminant APIs, and analytic j/\allowbreak{}elliptic APIs.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Noncomputable boundary audit & Any noncomputable pressure must be isolated to mirror/\allowbreak{}comparison layer or trigger a natural refactor;\newline it cannot leak into operational row generation.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Mathlib mitigation/\allowbreak{}refactor layer & Prefer executable finite constructors and proof-carrying row records. Use mirror layer for analytic/\allowbreak{}algebraic-irrational targets.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Bridge gap status & This closes a planning gap: v32 had specialized lattice-test phases, but lacked a general early test-matrix obligation for all computable paths.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Natural backjump/\allowbreak{}refactor layer & If a future test requires a duplicate generator or hand-coded special branch logic, backjump to the canonical generator layer or module manifest instead of patching in the test module.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Implementation prompt contract & Before implementing a computation phase, specify the generic test matrix rows, code-defined input generator, finite instance coverage, expected executable witness, and sentinel rows separately.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Global no-go inheritance & yes;\newline all global\_no\_go\_guardrails inherited (GNG01-GNG24)\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
How implemented & Added as a planning/\allowbreak{}infrastructure contract. It generalizes the v32 Eisenstein/\allowbreak{}Gauss test idea: those rows become sentinel cases in a broader finite test matrix over the code-defined generator/\allowbreak{}branching API, static/\allowbreak{}dynamic branch modes, L\_max rows, and executable witnesses.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Verification /\allowbreak{} evidence & YAML validation target: phase inserted before active cursor;\newline no white predecessors before active cursor;\newline LEG29/\allowbreak{}O07 registered with proof dossiers;\newline workflow/\allowbreak{}global guardrail/\allowbreak{}format decisions updated.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Proof strategy & Planning proof: check that generic finite test-matrix obligations are required before special sentinel rows can close;\newline check that the phase/\allowbreak{}order does not feed heuristic data into generator semantics.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Build result & No Lean build. PDF generation only after table regeneration.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Post-implementation plan check & After the first code-change phase, review whether generic finite tests caught an architectural error and whether the harness should be flattened for later phases.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Artifact packaging rule & Return full zip;\newline do not claim Lean build success unless executed locally/\allowbreak{}currently.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Implementation notes & This is not a mathematical theorem and not a Lean implementation. It prevents the first computation phases from becoming special-case demonstrations instead of general computable-path tests.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Source materials /\allowbreak{} references & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item v32 heuristic Eisenstein/\allowbreak{}Gauss test-matrix lock\item user clarification: early theory testing now, general rather than overspecialized\item existing canonical generator and smoke-test generator policy\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Extracted established theory objects & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item generic finite instance row\item static branching mode\item dynamic branching mode\item L\_max sweep row\item expected executable witness\item sentinel row for Eisenstein/\allowbreak{}Gauss only after generic coverage exists\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Modified modules & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item masters/\allowbreak{}cnna\_roadmap\_master.yaml\item scripts/\allowbreak{}generate\_tables.py\item generated TeX/\allowbreak{}PDF views\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Forbidden patterns checked & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item no special-case-only test matrix\item no duplicate generator for testing\item no CM/\allowbreak{}j target as operational input\item no angle/\allowbreak{}lattice heuristic as branching definition\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
No-go guardrails & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item Do not use Eisenstein/\allowbreak{}Gauss as the whole test harness.\item Do not duplicate generator logic for tests.\item Do not turn mirror/\allowbreak{}CM/\allowbreak{}j columns into operational inputs.\item Do not promote a computable path whose only passing examples are hand-picked sentinels.\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
\end{longtable}

\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Expected tactics & explicit structures, constructor/\allowbreak{}projection lemmas, ring-normal-form only for coordinate arithmetic, no Matrix.inv and no consumer-side patch.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]

\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Canonical generator policy & The table generator is the canonical renderer of phase-object link semantics.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Module compactness rule & Keep policy in the generator and YAML fields;\newline avoid a separate manual table source.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Refactor regression protocol & View regression: no unqualified blank Linked objects cell may remain after table generation.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Refactor migration metrics & Policies assigned to every phase;\newline direct/\allowbreak{}scope\_only/\allowbreak{}no\_direct\_object/\allowbreak{}moved counts recorded in report.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Lean/\allowbreak{}mathlib version policy & not applicable\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
ExecComplex boundary policy & not applicable\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Consumer-contract direction & Planning views consume object register and phase hierarchy;\newline object register does not consume table-rendered child scope.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Governance-overhead review & Justified because the prior table display could falsely suggest missing objects or broken phase coverage.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Proof rehearsal before implementation & Show that every phase has one of four cases: direct, scope\_only, no\_direct\_object, moved. Each case has a different rendering and validator obligation.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Expected tactics & Python generator validation;\newline YAML structural audit;\newline no Lean tactics.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Expected mathlib imports & none\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
ExecComplex/\allowbreak{}computable plan & not applicable;\newline planning infrastructure only\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Concrete computable instance traversal & Generator run over all phases/\allowbreak{}objects is the concrete planning instance.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Computable path non-skeleton status & not applicable to mathematical computation;\newline generator validation is concrete over the current master.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Expected executable witness & python scripts/\allowbreak{}generate\_tables.py produces policy-aware phase table and TSV exports.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Mathlib complication log & none\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Noncomputable boundary audit & none;\newline no Lean code\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Mathlib mitigation/\allowbreak{}refactor layer & none\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Bridge gap status & closes the display/\allowbreak{}ownership gap that made scope-only phases look empty\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Natural backjump/\allowbreak{}refactor layer & If a phase unexpectedly loses all direct objects, update the object Phase assignment or declare the correct non-direct policy before generating views.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Implementation prompt contract & Before code work, verify that the phase has a clear object-link policy and that the module manifest references direct or child-scope objects correctly.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Global no-go inheritance & yes;\newline all global\_no\_go\_guardrails inherited (GNG01-GNG24)\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
How implemented & Planning generator and YAML schema updated: phases carry Object link policy/\allowbreak{}explanation/\allowbreak{}child scope;\newline phase table renders direct objects, scope-only child summaries, no-direct-object explanations, or moved placeholders.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Verification /\allowbreak{} evidence & YAML has explicit policy fields for every phase;\newline generator validates allowed policy kinds and renders the phase table without raw blank Linked objects cells.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Proof strategy & Treat phase-object display as a generated invariant: empty direct-object sets require explicit phase policy, not silence.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Build result & PDF view generated;\newline no Lean build executed.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Post-implementation plan check & Future generator runs must fail or visibly explain if a phase has no direct linked object.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Artifact packaging rule & Return complete zip and PDF view;\newline do not claim Lean build.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Implementation notes & This is planning/\allowbreak{}documentation infrastructure only. It does not change Lean code and does not alter object ownership. Direct ownership remains controlled exclusively by the object register Phase field.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Source materials /\allowbreak{} references & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item v37 phase table linked-object ambiguity\item object register Phase field semantics\item phase hierarchy/\allowbreak{}child phase ownership\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Extracted established theory objects & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item LEG34 Phase-object-link policy contract\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Implemented modules & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item masters/\allowbreak{}cnna\_roadmap\_master.yaml\item scripts/\allowbreak{}generate\_tables.py\item tables/\allowbreak{}phase\_table.tex\item tables/\allowbreak{}phase\_object\_link\_policy.tex\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]

\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]

\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
No-go guardrails & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item No blank Linked objects cell without explicit policy\item No umbrella phase may claim child objects as direct ownership\item No object may be moved by display aggregation alone\end{itemize}\\

\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Reusable existing modules & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item phase\_object\_map\item phase hierarchy validator\item object register Phase field\end{itemize}\\

Concrete computable instance traversal & Planning-level concrete instance: trace a finite subsystem/\allowbreak{}cut -> boundary -> complement/\allowbreak{}regime -> Schur/\allowbreak{}DtN -> derived consumer chain for later active phases.\\

\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Bridge gap status & closes the ontic-primitivity gap: prevents later phases from treating physics/\allowbreak{}dynamics/\allowbreak{}arithmetic/\allowbreak{}geometry/\allowbreak{}environment as inputs.\\

Natural backjump/\allowbreak{}refactor layer & If a consumer needs a non-subsystem structure without provenance, backjump to the cut/\allowbreak{}regime/\allowbreak{}complement/\allowbreak{}Schur source layer rather than patching the consumer.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Implementation prompt contract & Before implementing any phase, state which structures are primitive, derived, or comparison-only. Only subsystem-being may be primitive.\\

\subsection*{Phase 1.1.1.1.1 -- Implementation module manifest and semantic placement gate}
\label{impl:phase-1-1-1-1-1}
\par\smallskip\noindent\begin{minipage}{0.99\linewidth}\footnotesize\raggedright\textbf{Table legend.} \colorbox{green!18}{\strut G} = green/closed and derived-only; \colorbox{white}{\strut W} = white/non-direct-Lean tooling/refactor/infrastructure/documentation/comparison; \colorbox{orange!28}{\strut O} = orange/implementable after stated predecessor closure; \colorbox{yellow!25}{\strut Y} = yellow/active and implementable now; \colorbox{red!20}{\strut R} = red/locked Lean-derivation phase; release requires derived-only object/proof evidence. Context class is orthogonal to status.\end{minipage}\par\vspace{0.25em}
\begin{longtable}{L{0.16\linewidth} L{0.81\linewidth}}
\hline
\textbf{Field} & \textbf{Record}\\
\hline
Phase status & \cellcolor{green!18}G\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Context class & LeanGovernancePhase\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Phase documentation tier & F1\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Phase documentation depth requirement & Extended dossier: definition, source chain, main lemmas/\allowbreak{}proof obligations, dependency risks, and downstream consumers. Enough detail for later implementation without re-reading chat history.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Record change version & \\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Record change timestamp & \\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Implementation status & completed\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Preparation status & completed\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Phase type & infrastructure\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Phase-type-specific contract & Infrastructure/\allowbreak{}planning phase: justify absence of operational witness;\newline close schema/\allowbreak{}decision prerequisites before code.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Title /\allowbreak{} state & Implementation module manifest and semantic placement gate\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Preparation workflow & Active module-manifest gate after global guardrail inheritance lock.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Source-to-CNNA mapping & LEG10 governs all later implementation phases. It is not mathematics;\newline it is a provenance/\allowbreak{}API hygiene contract preventing code fragmentation.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Dependency-depth audit & Depends on green parents 1.1.1.1, 1.1.1.1.0, and decision locks 1.1.1.1.0.1-1.1.1.1.0.13. No implementation may start until module names/\allowbreak{}budget, phase\_type, regression mode, version impact, consumer direction, concrete-instance status, and mathlib complication status are explicit.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Phase split decision & New child gate inserted before 1.1.1.1.2 and 1.1.1.1.3. No further split unless a separate generated manifest table becomes necessary.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Positive path plan & Define the manifest: for each future implementation phase, list proposed added modules, modified modules, exact names, semantic placement, phase\_type, type-specific obligations, reuse/\allowbreak{}extension/\allowbreak{}rejection decision, public/\allowbreak{}legacy/\allowbreak{}API role, import direction, regression protocol, version impact, and expected downstream consumers. Include a rule that any future bridge theorem must list whether it is an upstream generator input, downstream consumer, or comparison-only diagnostic. Additionally, every proposed computation module must include a generic finite test-matrix placement decision: whether it reuses O07, extends the canonical smoke-test generator, or documents why no executable test applies yet.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Module implementation manifest & Closed contract: every scratchpad record must expose proposed\_added\_modules, proposed\_modified\_modules, reusable\_existing\_modules, semantic placement, rejection/\allowbreak{}fit decision, public/\allowbreak{}legacy/\allowbreak{}API role, import direction, expected downstream consumers, phase\_type obligations, refactor regression protocol, version impact, concrete-instance traversal, generic test placement, mathlib complication log, and noncomputable boundary audit before Lean edits.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Semantic placement decision & The manifest is planning infrastructure and belongs to the single YAML master plus generated views. It is not a Lean module and must not be implemented as phase-local code. Future code modules are created only when an existing semantic module cannot be safely extended or wrapped.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Reuse and duplication audit & Phase 1.1.1.1.1 names reusable candidates but defers semantic generator classification to 1.1.1.1.2. Duplicate implementation remains blocked already at manifest level.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Canonical generator policy & Any generator-like phase must consume this manifest, then pass through 1.1.1.1.2 before Lean code. The manifest table must state whether O07/\allowbreak{}canonical smoke-test generator is reused, extended, or rejected.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Module compactness rule & Closed rule: phase granularity is not file granularity. A later phase must justify every new module by semantic ownership, API/\allowbreak{}firewall direction, or unavoidable import split.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Refactor regression protocol & This gate does not refactor code, but it must require every later refactor manifest to declare wrapper-first/\allowbreak{}incremental vs atomic mode. Default for 1.1.2 is wrapper-first incremental with local full-build evidence per package.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Refactor migration metrics & The active gate must define the metric template, not fill counts yet: declarations\_migrated\_count;\newline modules\_added/\allowbreak{}modified;\newline imports\_changed;\newline import\_graph\_delta;\newline consumer\_breakage\_audit;\newline compatibility aliases;\newline build evidence source.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Lean/\allowbreak{}mathlib version policy & Every module manifest must state whether it stays on Lean 4.28.0/\allowbreak{}mathlib baseline, uses no mathlib change, or requires a separate upgrade gate.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
ExecComplex boundary policy & EXEC-2/\allowbreak{}3/\allowbreak{}4 enforced: rational QxQ floor;\newline exact quadratic layer is required later for CM/\allowbreak{}lattice coordinates but may only be instantiated from a proof-carrying discriminant ledger;\newline analytic Complex and CD C-surfaces are not early scalar sources.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Consumer-contract direction & Every proposed public wrapper must state its expected consumers and import direction. BoundaryOperatorProfile must prepare for Phase 4.1 without importing Phase 4. For BR08/\allowbreak{}CD8 specifically, earlier route modules export data downstream and must not import the bridge.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Governance-overhead review & This gate is retained because it blocks concrete observed failure modes: one-phase-one-file proliferation, duplicate generator surfaces, hidden import direction, and unclassified mathlib/\allowbreak{}noncomputable pressure. Review flattening only after the first real code/\allowbreak{}refactor package closes.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Proof rehearsal before implementation & For a future phase, rehearse: target object -> existing module semantic scan -> either extend existing module or justify new module -> record consumer role -> only then implement.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Expected tactics & No Lean tactics expected. If mirrored as Lean audit constants later, use records with exact/\allowbreak{}rfl fields only.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Expected mathlib imports & None. This planning gate uses generated repo inventory, not new mathlib imports.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
ExecComplex/\allowbreak{}computable plan & No ExecComplex object introduced. The rule applies to future ExecComplex-touching phases: reuse existing boundary modules unless semantic placement proves otherwise.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Concrete computable instance traversal & This active gate is infrastructure-only: it will not introduce operational Lean code. For every later operational phase, the manifest must include a concrete finite instance path from generated input to intended endpoint. This phase must plan generic finite test traversal, not only special sentinels, for every upcoming computable path.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Computable path non-skeleton status & active infrastructure gate;\newline it prevents later skeleton-only operational phases by requiring concrete instance traversal fields in every scratchpad.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Expected executable witness & For this gate: generated manifest fields. For later operational phases: a named concrete finite generated instance and executable endpoint object.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Mathlib complication log & No new mathlib import in this gate. Later manifests must list expected mathlib imports and whether each is executable, noncomputable-only, classical-only, or mirror-layer-only. Scalar architecture check added: any proposed new module must classify Complex/\allowbreak{}Real/\allowbreak{}QuadraticAlgebra dependencies under LEG31. Derived-quadratic update: any proposed QuadraticAlgebra/\allowbreak{}AdjoinRoot/\allowbreak{}ExactQuadratic module must state whether it is a schema, D-instantiation, proof/\allowbreak{}mirror reference, or executable coordinate layer.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Noncomputable boundary audit & No Lean operational code changed. Later phases must document noncomputable leakage risk before implementation.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Mathlib mitigation/\allowbreak{}refactor layer & If a proposed implementation needs noncomputable mathlib APIs, the manifest must route them to an allowed mirror/\allowbreak{}non-operational module or backjump to an operational replacement layer.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Bridge gap status & No mathematical bridge. Missing semantic placement data blocks implementation, not mathematics.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Natural backjump/\allowbreak{}refactor layer & If implementation wants a new module but semantics fit an existing module, backjump to the existing module or neutral wrapper seam. If import direction prevents reuse, backjump to the earliest natural API/\allowbreak{}firewall layer.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]

\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Expected tactics & No Lean tactics expected. If proof-dossier records later get mirrored in Lean, use explicit structures and exact/\allowbreak{}rfl fields;\newline no simp-driven folding.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]

\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
ExecComplex/\allowbreak{}computable plan & No ExecComplex object introduced. For ExecComplex-touching objects, the dossier must distinguish operational computable path from mirror/\allowbreak{}non-operational interpretation.\\

\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Bridge gap status & Infrastructure gap closed. Remaining no/\allowbreak{}partial proof dossiers are object-local documentation/\allowbreak{}proof gaps and must be filled at their natural correction/\allowbreak{}implementation phases;\newline they do not block the infrastructure gate itself.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Natural backjump/\allowbreak{}refactor layer & If an object cannot be documented from its supposed sources, backjump to the earliest natural source object or blue-correction phase, not to the consumer/\allowbreak{}export layer.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Implementation prompt contract & Use the standardized positive-path implementation workflow and additionally update proof-dossier status for every object introduced, corrected, exported, or consumed as a public proof-relevant size.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Global no-go inheritance & yes;\newline all global\_no\_go\_guardrails inherited (GNG01-GNG24)\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
How implemented & Closed in v42 by turning the existing proofbook view into a stricter generated infrastructure gate. scripts/\allowbreak{}generate\_tables.py now validates one proof-dossier row per object ID, non-empty required dossier fields, object-register proof-documentation status, exact Proof dossier links of the form proof:<ID>, and matching object names/\allowbreak{}phases between the object register and object\_proof\_dossiers. The YAML master was normalized to satisfy those invariants. No Lean source was changed.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Verification /\allowbreak{} evidence & python3 scripts/\allowbreak{}generate\_tables.py succeeded with 189 phases, 199 objects, 189 scratch-pad records, and 199 proof-dossier records. v42 PDF compilation succeeded with pdflatex. Rendered PDF pages around the object/\allowbreak{}proof-documentation region were inspected. No new Lean build was run or claimed;\newline the user-reported green build is the Lean baseline.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Proof strategy & No Lean proof. Structural validation path: object register IDs -> expected proof:<ID> link -> matching object\_proof\_dossiers row -> generated anchor/\allowbreak{}table/\allowbreak{}export -> PDF view. Mathematical proof content remains per-object and code-checked in later natural phases.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Build result & Lean not run in this environment. User reported the pre-phase Lean build green;\newline v42 generator and PDF build succeeded.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Post-implementation plan check & Closed. Generated object tables contain proof-documentation status and dossier links for every object or quantity;\newline object\_proof\_dossiers coverage is generator-validated. Active cursor proceeds to 1.1.1.1.4 declaration classification finalization.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Artifact packaging rule & Return a complete zip with all modified files and generated planning artifacts when applicable. Lean build success may be recorded only from an actual current build or from the user-reported local build;\newline otherwise state that local Lean build is required.\\

Implementation notes & This closes only the documentation infrastructure. It does not mark all mathematical objects as proved. \hline\addlinespace[0.20em]

Module implementation manifest & No Lean module added now. Old modules remain as facades initially.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Semantic placement decision & The audit belongs after declaration consumption classification and before blue-object content correction. This is the natural layer because it decides public/\allowbreak{}import surfaces before new semantic content is added.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Reuse and duplication audit & RD08 records the consolidation decision rule. Same-semantics wrappers may be unified;\newline proof-stage modules must remain separated until a stronger canonical proof module preserves their role.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Canonical generator policy & No generator change. If consolidation touches generated Smoke files later, it must use the existing canonical generated-source/\allowbreak{}main-path seams and must not create a second generator path.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Module compactness rule & Slim modules only when statement/\allowbreak{}provenance auditability is preserved or improved. Small clone wrappers are candidates;\newline long proof chains are not automatically candidates;\newline a monolithic notation facade may require splitting rather than merging.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Refactor regression protocol & Mandatory before any Lean merge: retain old modules as facades in pass 1;\newline record declarations\_migrated\_count, imports\_changed, import\_graph\_delta, consumer\_breakage\_audit, legacy/\allowbreak{}coexistence status, and local full-build evidence.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Refactor migration metrics & Not yet executed. Initial audit candidates: BoundarySource four substrate ledger clones;\newline Handoff three A\_to\_\{C,D,E\} output aliases;\newline Handoff four \{B,C,D,E\}\_to\_A feedback-input clones;\newline no-merge candidates: SM generated proof chains, Cayley-Dickson S6 chain;\newline watch item: Arithmetic/\allowbreak{}Notation monolith.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Lean/\allowbreak{}mathlib version policy & Baseline remains Lean 4.28.0/\allowbreak{}mathlib snapshot. No new import may be assumed;\newline refactor-only changes must compile locally before closeout.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
ExecComplex boundary policy & EXEC-2 remains unchanged. Consolidation may not move rational executable data behind noncomputable or analytic mirrors.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Consumer-contract direction & Audit flows from code producers/\allowbreak{}import surfaces to consumers. If a consumer needs a consolidated API, the facade must be created at the producer/\allowbreak{}natural source layer, not patched in the consumer.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Governance-overhead review & This gate is justified because concrete clone families exist, but it must remain compact: one ledger, no broad rewrite, no full code normalization before the active content path.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Proof rehearsal before implementation & For each candidate cluster prove/\allowbreak{}check: same semantic operation, same or explicitly mapped public declarations, no consumer lost, import direction preserved or improved, natural generating layer chosen, old imports kept through facades, full local build green, and statement/\allowbreak{}provenance granularity not lost.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Expected tactics & If later Lean facades are created, expect explicit abbrev/\allowbreak{}def wrappers, namespace-preserving aliases, extensionality reuse where existing, and rfl proofs. Avoid tactic-heavy rewrites;\newline no @[simp] promotion in operational path.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Expected mathlib imports & No new mathlib imports expected for audit. Any later facade/\allowbreak{}refactor should reuse existing imports;\newline new imports require Lean 4.28.0 verification.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
ExecComplex/\allowbreak{}computable plan & No ExecComplex changes in the audit. If Arithmetic/\allowbreak{}Notation or operational modules are touched later, preserve all concrete executable witnesses and rational-boundary policy.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Concrete computable instance traversal & Not a computation phase. Concrete evidence is the finite repository module/\allowbreak{}declaration graph plus direct code spot-checks of named files. Later executable refactors must preserve existing executable witnesses.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Computable path non-skeleton status & Audit-only. A later refactor may not skeletonize operational paths;\newline it must preserve concrete generated source, DtN/\allowbreak{}Schur, determinant, and discriminant witnesses.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Expected executable witness & For audit: generated module inventory and candidate report. For later code refactor: unchanged downstream Lean build plus regenerated module/\allowbreak{}declaration inventory showing no lost public declarations unless explicitly facaded.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Mathlib complication log & No mathlib complication in audit. Later code movement may expose import-minimization issues;\newline resolve by preserving existing import surfaces first.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Noncomputable boundary audit & No noncomputable introduced. Later operational refactor cannot introduce noncomputable/\allowbreak{}classical pressure into executable paths.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Mathlib mitigation/\allowbreak{}refactor layer & If new imports are needed, prefer local facade modules or existing imported modules rather than widening mathlib dependency surface.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Bridge gap status & Current bridge gap is governance-level: exact consolidation candidates are not yet fully ledgered. No mathematical bridge is missing. Do not patch consumers;\newline place any new facade at the natural source layer of the duplicate/\allowbreak{}schema family.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]

Governance-overhead review & This gate is justified because concrete clone families exist, but it must remain compact: one ledger, no broad rewrite, no full code normalization before the active content path.\\

\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]

Global no-go inheritance & yes;\newline all global\_no\_go\_guardrails inherited (GNG01-GNG26)\\

How implemented & Activated in P1.46 after user-reported green local lake build closed LEG44. Implementation has not started;\newline this phase must first produce a neutral naming/\allowbreak{}facade migration map, not a source refactor.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Verification /\allowbreak{} evidence & Inputs available: closed LEG42 notation-surface audit, closed LEG43 row-level removal review, closed/\allowbreak{}build-validated LEG44 notation-removal execution certificate, and semantic consolidation findings SCF44/\allowbreak{}SCF45/\allowbreak{}SCF52.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Proof strategy & Build the neutral migration map only after notation keep/\allowbreak{}quarantine/\allowbreak{}remove/\allowbreak{}rename decisions are fixed. Every rename/\allowbreak{}merge/\allowbreak{}split records notation/\allowbreak{}API impact.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Build result & No Lean build needed for activation;\newline source refactor/\allowbreak{}build validation belongs to LEG39 after LEG38 emits a concrete map.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Post-implementation plan check & Active cursor after P1.46. Close only when the migration map is explicit enough for bounded LEG39 refactor execution.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Artifact packaging rule & Return a full zip with updated YAML, exports, report and generated docs;\newline do not claim Lean build.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Implementation notes & Active planning gate. No Lean modules should be moved/\allowbreak{}renamed yet. The required output is a migration map separating neutral canonical names, old-module facades, legacy quarantine, no-merge exceptions, and proof-stage exceptions.\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Source materials /\allowbreak{} references & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item LEG42 notation ledger TSV/\allowbreak{}JSON\item SCF74 unconsumed notation finding\item CNNA/\allowbreak{}PillarA/\allowbreak{}**/\allowbreak{}Notation.lean\item module import graph\item phase-semantic module-name audit\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Extracted established theory objects & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item LEG38\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Modified modules & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item Repository/\allowbreak{}masters/\allowbreak{}cnna\_roadmap\_master.yaml\item README.md\item Repository/\allowbreak{}archive/\allowbreak{}reports/\allowbreak{}V1\_21\_PRE\_CODE\_READINESS\_DERIVATION\_ORDER\_AUDIT\_REPORT.md\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Forbidden patterns checked & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item no Lean source move in the audit phase\item no merge by filename similarity alone\item no deletion of old import surfaces without facade migration\item no merging proof-stage chains that encode derivation/\allowbreak{}provenance order\item no new semantic truth layer from the audit ledger\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
No-go guardrails & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item GLOBAL-INHERITED: all global\_no\_go\_guardrails apply, especially GNG03, GNG21, GNG23-GNG26.\item no semantic merge without natural-layer placement\item no deletion of compatibility imports in the first refactor pass\item no proof-stage chain merge by naming similarity\item no code slimming that reduces statement/\allowbreak{}provenance auditability\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Reusable existing modules & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item CNNA/\allowbreak{}PillarA/\allowbreak{}Arithmetic/\allowbreak{}BoundarySource/\allowbreak{}GeneratedSubstrateRoute.lean\item CNNA/\allowbreak{}PillarA/\allowbreak{}Handoff/\allowbreak{}Core/\allowbreak{}SectorExport.lean\item CNNA/\allowbreak{}PillarA/\allowbreak{}Handoff/\allowbreak{}Outputs/\allowbreak{}Closed.lean\item repo\_inventory/\allowbreak{}raw\_export/\allowbreak{}*.json\item generated\_exports/\allowbreak{}consumption\_classification\_map.generated.tsv\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
Duplicate-risk symbols or operations & \begin{itemize}[leftmargin=*,itemsep=0.05em,topsep=0.05em]\item phase-semantic module names;\newline unclassified declarations;\newline duplicate wrapper modules;\newline stale object/\allowbreak{}proof mappings.\end{itemize}\\
\hline\addlinespace[0.20em]
\end{longtable}

Phase type & refactor\\
